originalTitle: Meta's stock sinks on report company could raise tens of billions of dollars to fund AI push
summary: Meta shares dropped after the Financial Times reported the company could potentially raise tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to help its AI push.
content:
Meta shares dropped more than 5% on Friday after the Financial Times reported that the company could potentially raise tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to fund its artificial intelligence investments.

The social media company is pondering the potential stock sale, the FT reported, after rival Alphabet said this week that it plans to raise $85 billion from selling equity, increasing that number from $80 billion. Alphabet has been on a tear over the past year, but the stock is down for a fourth straight week as investors worry about excessive AI spending.

Meta hasn't hired banks and may not issue new stock, the FT noted.

A Meta spokesperson called the report "pure speculation."

"We've been clear that huge opportunities lie ahead in AI, and we'll continue focusing on raising capital in the most flexible ways to support that," the spokesperson said in an email.

Like other tech giants, Meta and Alphabet are pouring record sums into capital expenditures as they race to build out AI infrastructure to meet what they see as insatiable demand.

In April, Meta raised its 2026 capex guidance to as high as $145 billion from a previous forecast of up to $135 billion. Alphabet said that month that it was hiking the top end of its capex guidance by $5 billion to $190 billion.

Wall Street has been treating the two companies very differently over the past year, largely because Alphabet has a prospering cloud business that helps justify its hefty spending. Alphabet's stock is up more than 115% in the last 12 months, topping all of its megacap peers, while Meta's stock is down 13%, the worst performer in the bunch.

WATCH : Evercore's Mark Mahaney: Meta is one of my favorite stocks in the large cap internet space .
chineseTitle: Meta 股价下跌，因有报道称该公司可能筹集数百亿美元资金推动 AI 发展
chineseSummary: 在《金融时报》报道该公司可能通过股票发行筹集数百亿美元以帮助其人工智能发展后，Meta 股价下跌。
chineseContent:
在《金融时报》报道称该公司可能通过股票发行筹集数百亿美元为其人工智能投资提供资金后，周五 Meta 股价下跌超过 5%。

据英国《金融时报》报道，这家社交媒体公司正在考虑潜在的股票出售，此前其竞争对手 Alphabet 本周表示，计划通过出售股权筹集 850 亿美元，这一数字从 800 亿美元增加到了 850 亿美元。 Alphabet 在过去一年中表现强劲，但由于投资者担心人工智能支出过多，该股已连续第四周下跌。

英国《金融时报》指出，Meta 尚未聘请银行，也可能不会发行新股。

Meta 发言人称该报告“纯粹是猜测”。

这位发言人在一封电子邮件中表示：“我们很清楚，人工智能领域存在着巨大的机遇，我们将继续专注于以最灵活的方式筹集资金来支持这一机遇。”

与其他科技巨头一样，Meta 和 Alphabet 正在向资本支出投入创纪录的资金，竞相建设人工智能基础设施，以满足他们认为无法满足的需求。

4 月份，Meta 将 2026 年资本支出指导从之前的 1,350 亿美元上调至 1,450 亿美元。 Alphabet 当月表示，将把资本支出指导上限提高 50 亿美元，达到 1900 亿美元。

过去一年，华尔街对这两家公司的态度截然不同，很大程度上是因为 Alphabet 拥有蓬勃发展的云业务，这有助于证明其巨额支出是合理的。 Alphabet 的股价在过去 12 个月内上涨了 115% 以上，超过了所有大型股同行，而 Meta 的股价则下跌了 13%，是这群股票中表现最差的。

观看：Evercore 的 Mark Mahaney：Meta 是我在大盘互联网领域最喜欢的股票之一。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
